html:
Exclusive to:

Lune Trees are Trees exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. They are found in the Lunar Forest and Lunar Baths Biome on the Lunar Island. Additional Lune Trees can also be found on the Hermit Island.

Chopping a small Lune Tree gives 2 Logs and 50% to get a Lune Tree Blossom, the resulting stumps can be dug for one Log.

Chopping a normal Lune Tree gives 3 Logs, 1 Lune Tree Blossom plus 25% to get an extra one, and spawn one Moon Moth. The resulting stumps can be dug for 2 Logs.

Chopping a tall Lune Tree gives 4 Logs, 2 Lune Tree Blossom and spawn one Moon Moth plus 50% to spawn another. The resulting stumps can be dug for 2 Logs.

Moon Moths can be caught with a Bug Net and planted to make a Lune Tree Sapling.

Tips

  • These can be a slightly better source for wood than Evergreens or Birchnut Trees as they yield up to 6 Logs instead of 4. Additionally, chopping down a Lune Tree does not risk spawning hostile creatures such as Treeguards or Poison Birchnut Trees.
  • Lune Trees can also be found on the Hermit Island. Since the island's location can be revealed through Messages in a Bottle, this may provide players with access to Lune Trees before locating the Lunar Island.


Trivia

  • Lune Trees were introduced in the Turn of Tides update.
  • Lune Trees are probably modeled after Adansonia Baobab or desert rose trees.
  • The fact that, similar to Butterflies, Moon Moths are used to plant Lune Tree implies that they're oversized Flowers, rather than actual trees.

Gallery

  • All growth stages of the Lune Tree.
  • A Lune Tree Sapling.
  • Burnt Lune Trees.
  • Lune Tree Stumps Burnt
  • Lune Trees as found in the Lunar Forest.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

“I didn't know trees grew on the moon.”
–Wilson
“It's so pretty, I might actually feel bad burning it!”
–Willow
“Roly poly weird tree.”
–Wolfgang
“A fleeting beauty.”
–Wendy
“I HAVE SEEN SO MANY TREES AT THIS POINT”
–WX-78
“It seems to have been altered by ambient lunar energies.”
–Wickerbottom
“Measure twice, chop once.”
–Woodie
“It's a moon tree.”
–Maxwell
“Tis lovely as Yggdrasil's picture in my mind.”
–Wigfrid
“What a pretty tree!”
–Webber
“What a strange looking tree.”
–Warly
“Friend likes Night Ball”
–Wormwood
“Sure is pretty, ain't it?”
–Winona
“Glimmering tree of lunar light.”
–Wortox
“Trees weird here.”
–Wurt
“That one definitely isn't in my handbook.”
–Walter
“It looks like it's already gone grey.”
–Wanda

{{pic24|Log}}×1 (small) |renew = Yes }} {{Quotes |wilson = I didn't know trees grew on the moon. |wigfrid = Tis lovely as Yggdrasil's picture in my mind. |warly = What a strange looking tree. |wormwood = Friend likes Night Ball |willow = It's so pretty, I might actually feel bad burning it! |woodie = Measure twice, chop once. |webber = What a pretty tree! |winona = Sure is pretty, ain't it? |maxwell = It's a moon tree. |wolfgang = Roly poly weird tree. |wendy = A fleeting beauty. |wurt = Trees weird here. |wickerbottom = It seems to have been altered by ambient lunar energies. |wortox = Glimmering tree of lunar light. |wx78 = I HAVE SEEN SO MANY TREES AT THIS POINT |walter = That one definitely isn't in my handbook. |wanda = It looks like it's already gone grey. |choose = wigfrid }} '''Lune Trees''' are [[Tree]]s exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[Return of Them]]''. They are found in the [[Lunar Forest]] and [[Lunar Baths]] [[Biomes|Biome]] on the [[Lunar Island]]. Additional Lune Trees can also be found on the [[Hermit Island]]. Chopping a small Lune Tree gives 2 [[Log]]s and 50% to get a [[Lune Tree Blossom]], the resulting stumps can be dug for one Log. Chopping a normal Lune Tree gives 3 Logs, 1 Lune Tree Blossom plus 25% to get an extra one, and spawn one [[Moon Moth]]. The resulting stumps can be dug for 2 Logs. Chopping a tall Lune Tree gives 4 Logs, 2 Lune Tree Blossom and spawn one Moon Moth plus 50% to spawn another. The resulting stumps can be dug for 2 Logs. Moon Moths can be caught with a [[Bug Net]] and planted to make a Lune Tree Sapling. == {{pic|32|Prototype}} Tips == * These can be a slightly better source for wood than [[Evergreen]]s or [[Birchnut Tree]]s as they yield up to 6 Logs instead of 4. Additionally, chopping down a Lune Tree does not risk spawning hostile creatures such as Treeguards or Poison Birchnut Trees. * Lune Trees can also be found on the Hermit Island. Since the island's location can be revealed through [[Message in a Bottle/DST|Messages in a Bottle]], this may provide players with access to Lune Trees before locating the Lunar Island. == {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * Lune Trees were introduced in the ''[[Return of Them#Turn of Tides|Turn of Tides]]'' update. * Lune Trees are probably modeled after [[wikipedia:Adansonia Baobab|Adansonia Baobab]] or [[wikipedia:Adenium obesum|desert rose]] trees. * The fact that, similar to [[Butterfly|Butterflies]], Moon Moths are used to plant Lune Tree implies that they're oversized [[Flower]]s, rather than actual trees. {{clr}} == {{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery == Lune Tree stages.png|All growth stages of the Lune Tree.
